Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

We reviewed the retrieved evidence and found clear confirmation that Li-Fraumeni syndrome is indeed a rare hereditary disorder, primarily caused by mutations in the TP53 gene. Crucially, it is inherited in an autosomal dominant fashion—not recessive—as stated explicitly in multiple references (e.g., entry [10]). Although some cases might lack identifiable TP53 mutations, the established mode of inheritance remains autosomal dominant. Thus, describing it as autosomal recessive is factually inaccurate.
